factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with a significant increase in cash from operating activities compared to the prior year.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities in the first three months of 2018 of $18.1 million...

factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, supported by increased unit sales and revenue growth.

evidence.text: Net sales were $101.2 million and $79.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017 , respectively, an increase of $22.0 million or 27.7% .

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment.

evidence.text: Bookings related to the Company's Software and Sensors segment, net of cancellations, were $97.5 million and $60.1 million during the three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017 , respectively, an increase of $37.4 million , or 62.3% .

factors.summary: The company is holding prices, with no evidence of discounts or forced price adjustments.

evidence.text: The Company increased sales of its TASER X26P and X2 Smart Weapons by $5.8 million to $40.4 million during the quarter ended March 31, 2018 as compared to $34.7 million during the same period in 2017 , which was primarily attributable to increased sales under the Officer Safety Plan and TASER 60 payment programs.

factors.summary: The Software and Sensors segment is growing fastest, driven by increased adoption of on-officer cameras and digital evidence management software.

evidence.text: The overall increase in the Software and Sensors segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable, with no significant changes in variable manufacturing costs.

evidence.text: The Company noted no significant changes in variable manufacturing costs during the three months ended March 31, 2018 as compared to the same period in 2017 .
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, particularly in the TASER Weapons segment, due to higher salaries and bonuses for sales and marketing, and professional staff.

evidence.text: Within the TASER Weapons segment, SG&A increased $4.0 million , or 23.5% , to $21.3 million during the three months ended March 31, 2018 as compared to $17.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017 . Of the increase, $2.1 million related to higher salaries, benefits and bonus related primarily to sales and marketing, professional staff and general support staff, as well as increased professional and consulting costs of $1.5 million primarily related to its implementation efforts related to the adoption of Topic 606.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable, with no significant disruptions mentioned.

evidence.text: The Company noted no significant changes in variable manufacturing costs during the three months ended March 31, 2018 as compared to the same period in 2017 .
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing, driven by higher sales volumes in both segments.

evidence.text: Cost of product sales increased to $20.5 million for the three months ended March 31, 2018 from $18.0 million for the same period in 2017 as a result of higher sales volumes, and increased as a percentage of sales to 32.3% from 31.3% , respectively.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Product and Service Sales

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capacity utilization indicates higher efficiency and production, which is a positive sign for supply chain operations.

factors.summary: Management has not explicitly raised or lowered guidance, but expects long-term margin improvements through scale.

evidence.text: The Company expects that these investments will result in an increase in our subscription revenue base, which over time will result in revenue increasing faster than the increase in selling, general and administrative expenses and research and development costs, as we reach economies of scale. With this increased operating leverage, the Company expects its operating margins to increase in the long term.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expectation of long-term margin improvements suggests a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as indicated by investments in R&D and expansion into new markets.

evidence.text: The Company expects R&D expense to continue to increase in absolute dollars as it invests in the deployment of new CEW technologies and focuses on growing the Software and Sensors segment as it adds headcount and additional resources to develop new products and services to further advance its scalable cloud-connected device platform.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investments in R&D and new markets indicate confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential budget appropriations and contract cancellations affecting bookings, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment.

evidence.text: Although the Company has entered into contracts for the delivery of products and services in the future and anticipates the contracts will be fulfilled, if agencies do not exercise contractual options, do not appropriate funds in future year budgets, or do enact a cancellation clause, revenue associated with these bookings may not ultimately be recognized, resulting in a future reduction to bookings.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential budget issues and contract cancellations pose risks to near-term bookings.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is clear and well-resourced, focusing on subscription-based models and expanding into international markets.

evidence.text: Our long-term financial strategy includes shifting our revenue, contracts, and cash flows from book-and-ship hardware transactions to multi-element, multi-year, subscription or recurring payment plans. [...] The Company is also highly focused on driving operating leverage and profitability within our two reported segments, TASER Weapons and Software & Sensors.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear, well-resourced strategy focusing on subscriptions and international expansion is positive.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include government budgets, which can impact the public safety market, and the ability to leverage fixed costs in the cloud.

evidence.text: The Company's long-term financial strategy includes shifting our revenue, contracts, and cash flows from book-and-ship hardware transactions to multi-element, multi-year, subscription or recurring payment plans. [...] The increase in gross margin was primarily attributable to increased leveraging of fixed costs related to cloud storage.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivities to government budgets and cloud leverage are important but not clearly positive or negative without more context.

factors.summary: A specific catalyst is the continued adoption of the company's cloud-based software solutions, which is driving growth in the Software and Sensors segment.

evidence.text: The overall increase in the Software and Sensors segment was driven by continued adoption of on-officer cameras and related technologies, including the Company's Evidence.com digital evidence management software suite.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Adoption of cloud-based software is a catalyst for growth in the Software and Sensors segment.

factors.summary: Unit volumes show mixed performance across product lines, with notable growth in the Software and Sensors segment, particularly in products like Axon Body, Axon Flex, Axon Fleet, Axon Dock, and TASER Cam, while other products experience declines.

evidence.text: Net unit sales for TASER Weapons handles and other products and Software and Sensors segment products were as follows: TASER X26P 15,720 (32.7%)...

factors.summary: The company is experiencing strong organic revenue growth, with net sales increasing year-over-year and accelerating performance across both product and service segments. The growth rate has notably increased, reflecting a positive trend in the business.

evidence.text: Net sales by product line were as follows (dollars in thousands): Total net sales $101,215 (6.9%)...
Net sales were $101.2 million and $79.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017 , respectively, an increase of $22.0 million or 27.7% .

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong organic revenue growth with increasing net sales and accelerating performance across segments.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a positive margin trajectory, with both gross and operating margins showing stability and expansion. The anticipation of future income growth and increased leveraging of fixed costs contribute to this upward trend. However, the realization of deferred tax assets depends on maintaining sufficient pre-tax book income.

evidence.text: The effective tax rate was favorably impacted by a $1.6 million discrete tax benefit...

Gross margin increased $15.8 million to $64.5 million for the three months ended March 31, 2018 compared to $48.7 million for the same period in 2017. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin was 63.7% for the three months ended March 31, 2018 compared to 61.4% for the same period in 2017, which was primarily attributable to increased leveraging of fixed costs related to cloud storage.
We anticipate the Company’s future income to continue to trend upward from our 2017 results, with sufficient pre-tax book income to realize a large portion of our deferred tax assets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Both gross and operating margins are improving, driven by increased leveraging of fixed costs.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting positively towards higher-margin segments, particularly the Software and Sensors segment, which is growing significantly faster than the TASER Weapons segment.

evidence.text: Total Software and Sensors segment 37,691 (37.2%)... compared to 30,242 (32.0%)...
Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment were $37.7 million and $21.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017 , respectively, an increase of $16.1 million , or 74.7% .

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifting positively towards higher-margin segments with significant growth in Software and Sensors.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction measures, particularly through managing excess and obsolete inventory quarterly, while also enhancing operational efficiency and leveraging fixed costs to improve margins. However, there is no explicit mention of broader cost-cutting initiatives beyond these specific actions.

evidence.text: During each of the three months ended March 31, 2018 and 2017, the Company recorded provisions for excess and obsolete inventory of $0.7 million...

The increase in service margins during the three months ended March 31, 2018 as compared to the same period in 2017 was attributable to the reduction of non-recurring expenses related to the Company's data migration to its new cloud-storage provider that was completed in 2018, as well as increased leveraging of fixed costs related to cloud-storage.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction measures through inventory management and operational efficiency improvements.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, driven by both increased sales of higher-margin products and enhanced efficiency in hardware and service margins.

evidence.text: The increase was primarily due to increased Evidence.com revenues driven by the continued increase in total users, as well as increases in sales of the Company's camera and related hardware products.

Within the Software and Sensors segment, cost of product and service sales increased to $16.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2018 from $12.5 million for the same period in 2017 as a result of higher sales volumes, and decreased as a percentage of sales to 43.0% for the three months ended March 31, 2018 from 58.2% for the same period in 2017.
